generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides real-time temperature data for building environmental monitoring and control, integrated into the DT framework for dynamic adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides real-time humidity data for building environmental monitoring and control, integrated into the DT framework for dynamic adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides real-time occupancy data to monitor building usage patterns and support energy-efficient control strategies."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides real-time data on broader environmental parameters (e.g., air quality, light levels) to support comprehensive building monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

instance EquipmentStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides real-time status data for building equipment (e.g., HVAC, lighting systems) to enable predictive maintenance and operational insights."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor
]

instance EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EquipmentStatusSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Foundational spatial representation of the physical building infrastructure derived from BIM, serving as the geometric basis for the DT."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimPlatformEnabler
]

instance BimBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM model defining sensors, actuators, and controllers (BCS components) with semantic interoperability and dynamic control logic."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimPlatformEnabler, ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler
]

instance RealTimeBuildingData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuously synchronized operational data from physical building systems, enabling bidirectional updates between physical and digital representations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalDataTransmitted, EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance StructuredBimData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Automatically extracted structured representations from enriched BIM models, ensuring alignment between physical and digital systems."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BimPlatformEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Foundation for device modeling and control logic definition, addressing BIM's static limitations by integrating dynamic BCS components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceModelingService, ModelDrivenDeploymentService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Core framework for automated transformation of enriched BIM models into deployable DT artifacts, ensuring semantic consistency and minimal manual intervention."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelDrivenDeploymentService, CustomServiceDevelopmentService
]

instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Specialized technology for real-time IoT data ingestion, enabling bidirectional synchronization between physical and digital systems with low-latency communication."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataExchangeService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin state management system facilitating real-time synchronization and operational parity between physical and digital representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataExchangeService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Provides rich, interactive visualization interfaces for immersive monitoring and control operations within the DT framework."
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DeviceModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables semantic definition of BCS components (sensors, actuators, controllers) within BIM, creating a unified semantic foundation for subsequent transformations."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceConfigurationInsight
]

instance RealTimeDataExchangeService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Facilitates continuous synchronization between physical building and digital twin via bidirectional data streams, ensuring operational parity for monitoring and control."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeSynchronizationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ClosedLoopControlAction
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides immersive interfaces for monitoring system states, visualizing building operations, and interacting with the DT to operate physical infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateVisualizationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlAction
]

instance CustomServiceDevelopmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supports modular extension of DT functionality through custom services and plugins, leveraging standardized architecture with predefined extension points."
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomAnalyticsInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceAction
]

instance ModelDrivenDeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implements MDE principles to automate life-cycle deployment of DTs from initial BIM-based modeling to runtime operation, reducing manual effort."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentAutomationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance DeviceConfigurationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic foundation for BCS components enabling automated extraction of structured representations from enriched BIM models."
]

instance RealTimeSynchronizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous bidirectional data synchronization ensuring operational parity between physical and digital building representations."
]

    base:desc "Immersive monitoring interface providing real-time visualization of building system states and operational parameters."
]

    base:desc "Integration of advanced analytics tools and algorithms to enhance decision support and operational efficiency."
]

instance ClosedLoopControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control logic integration enabling dynamic adjustments based on real-time sensor data and predefined control parameters."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RemoteControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Facility manager interface for remote operation of physical building infrastructure through the DT visualization platform."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated equipment health monitoring and predictive maintenance recommendations based on real-time operational data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DeploymentAutomationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Automated generation of deployable artifacts (simulation models, control logic) from enriched BIM data, reducing manual deployment effort."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HvacActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control system actuator for HVAC systems, dynamically adjusted based on real-time environmental data from the DT framework."
]

instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control system actuator for lighting systems, configured within BIM and controlled remotely through the DT interface."
]

    base:desc "Building control system actuator for ventilation systems, integrated into the DT framework for automated or manual control via digital twin interface."
]

// C10: Additional Models/Data
instance AnalyticalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Executable models generated via MDE principles for simulation, optimization, or real-time monitoring of building operations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic control logic definitions for building automation systems, semantically linked to BIM-derived models for operational consistency."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimPlatformEnabler
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT framework enables **control flows** from the digital twin to physical infrastructure via **Facility Managers**, who interact with the deployed DT for **real-time monitoring and control**. Control signals are generated based on operational insights derived from the synchronized digital model and fed back to the **Building Control System (BCS)**. The framework’s **modular architecture** supports service integration, allowing for dynamic extension of control capabilities. While the document does not specify command types (e.g., HVAC setpoints, lighting adjustments) or validation/authentication mechanisms, it implies a **bidirectional data exchange** role for the DT Framework as a central hub. Conflict resolution and command confirmation are not explicitly detailed, though the **runtime configuration management** suggests adaptability to operational changes without redeployment."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system in the AECO domain operates with a **real-time synchronization** paradigm, enabling continuous data exchange between the physical asset (e.g., a building) and its virtual representation to support **real-time insights** and **predictive analytics**. The temporal granularity is aligned with the operational needs of infrastructure management, where **dynamic updates** are critical for decision-making. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, min, or event-based triggers) are not specified, the emphasis on **real-time** implies low-latency requirements to maintain fidelity between the physical and digital domains. The system leverages runtime data integration to ensure temporal consistency across simulations, analyses, and optimization processes, though no explicit latency thresholds or granularity constraints (e.g., sub-second, hourly) are documented."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) **constellation** in the AECO domain is designed as a **cyber-physical system** where **static models** (e.g., BIM—Building Information Modeling) are **carefully integrated with runtime data** to create a **virtual representation** of physical assets. The **orchestration and coordination** of the DT system rely on **architecture patterns** that enable **data-driven infrastructure management**, including **real-time simulation, analysis, and optimization**. Key aspects include:
  - **Component Integration**: Seamless fusion of **static models** (e.g., geometric, structural, or functional models) with **dynamic runtime data** (e.g., IoT sensor feeds, operational logs, or environmental data) to ensure **temporal and spatial coherence**.
  - **Resource Management**: Efficient allocation of computational and data resources to support **predictive analytics** and **decision-making** across the building lifecycle (design, construction, operations, refurbishment).
  - **System-Wide Coordination**: Mechanisms to synchronize **heterogeneous data sources** and **fragmented models**, addressing challenges in **complex and large-scale infrastructure environments**. The document highlights the need for **systematic deployment** to avoid siloed DT instances, implying a **service-oriented or microservices-based architecture** for modularity and scalability.
  The **overall orchestration** must support **collaborative workflows** among stakeholders (e.g., architects, engineers, operators) while maintaining **consistency, traceability, and adaptability** to evolving infrastructure needs."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in this model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static BIM representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** within the BIM environment, creating a **semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. This phase leverages BIM’s static capabilities to model Building Control System (BCS) components, ensuring alignment with physical infrastructure.
  2. **Model Transformation**: The framework employs **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles** to extract **structured representations** from enriched BIM models, enabling **automated generation of deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention. This phase bridges the static BIM models and dynamic DT requirements.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, enabling **real-time data exchange** between the physical building and its digital twin, ensuring continuous synchronization.
  4. **Operation and Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to **monitor system states** and **operate physical infrastructure**, while the DT provides **immersive visualization interfaces** for monitoring and control.
  5. **Custom Service Integration**: DT Engineers extend functionality by developing and integrating **custom services and plugins** via standardized extension points, allowing the DT to evolve with operational needs. This phase emphasizes **modular architecture** and **runtime extensibility**.
  Transition management between phases is governed by the framework’s **modular architecture**, ensuring seamless handoffs between roles (e.g., Building Control Engineers → Facility Managers → DT Engineers) and supporting **lifecycle governance** through standardized extension points and automated artifact generation."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model Fidelity and Validity:**
  - **Model Fidelity Requirements:**
    - The framework addresses the gap between BIM’s static representations and the dynamic nature of DTs by supporting the complete life cycle of DT deployment and operation. This includes high-fidelity representations of devices (sensors, actuators, controllers) and their control logic within BIM.
    - Real-time synchronization ensures that the DT remains an accurate reflection of the physical building’s state.

  - **Validation and Verification Methods:**
    - Implicit in the framework’s design, where Building Control Engineers define and configure devices and control logic, which are then validated during DT deployment.
    - Custom services and plugins are integrated and tested to ensure they meet operational requirements.

  - **Uncertainty Sources and Quantification:**
    - Not explicitly detailed, but potential sources may include sensor inaccuracies, delays in data transmission, or discrepancies between the physical and digital models.
    - Quantification is likely managed through real-time synchronization and error detection mechanisms.

  - **Error Detection and Correction Mechanisms:**
    - Real-time data exchange and continuous synchronization help detect discrepancies between the physical and digital twins.
    - Interactive visualization interfaces allow Facility Managers to monitor system states and identify anomalies, enabling manual or automated corrections.

  - **Quality Assurance Processes:**
    - The framework’s modular architecture and standardized extension points support rigorous testing and validation of custom services and plugins.
    - Automated transformation and minimal manual intervention reduce human error during DT artifact generation.
    - Ongoing monitoring and operational feedback loops ensure continuous improvement and maintenance of model fidelity.
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT framework’s **technical network infrastructure** relies on **Eclipse Hono** for **IoT protocol adaptation** (e.g., MQTT, AMQP) and **Ditto** for **digital twin state synchronization**, ensuring **real-time bidirectional communication**. The **modular architecture** supports **standardized extension points** for integrating additional protocols (e.g., OPC UA, REST APIs) as needed. Network topology is **hybrid**, combining **edge computing** (for low-latency local processing) with **cloud-based coordination** (for global scalability). Bandwidth requirements are **adaptive**, prioritizing critical data streams (e.g., safety-critical alerts) via **quality-of-service (QoS) mechanisms**. Latency targets are implied by **real-time synchronization** needs but not explicitly quantified. **Reliability and redundancy** are addressed through the **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which include **scalability** and **maintainability**, though specific failover or backup protocols are not specified. **Security protocols** for network communication are not detailed beyond the framework’s emphasis on **interoperability** across heterogeneous systems."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT framework supports a **model-based deployment** process initiated by **Building Control Engineers**, who use BIM tools to perform **device modeling** and deploy the DT. The framework acts as a **central hub** for bidirectional data exchange, facilitating **holistic building life cycle management**. Deployment is enabled by **runtime configuration management**, allowing adjustments to operational requirements without full redeployment. While the document does not specify hosting platforms (cloud/edge/on-premise), it highlights **scalability** (from components to entire infrastructure) and **maintainability** (low-overhead updates) as key non-functional requirements (NF_X). Availability/reliability assurances are implied by the **interoperability** and **accessibility** requirements for users with varying technical expertise, but no explicit deployment models (e.g., hybrid, edge) or computational/storage specifications are provided."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system relies on **interoperability standards** and **data exchange protocols** to integrate with external systems, though specific technologies (e.g., IFC, COBie, OPC UA, MQTT) are not explicitly mentioned in the provided documents. The text highlights challenges arising from the **fragmented nature of models and data** in the AECO domain, necessitating robust integration frameworks to enable **collaboration across stakeholders** (e.g., architects, engineers, operators). The system’s architecture must accommodate **heterogeneous environments**, suggesting dependencies on **enterprise systems** or other Digital Twins for comprehensive lifecycle management. While no explicit protocols or standards are named, the context implies a need for **standardized data formats** and **service-oriented integration patterns** to avoid silos and ensure seamless data flow between physical assets, digital models, and external platforms."
]
